steps.title: Complete the square
steps.content:
Starting with $x^2 + 2x = 72$, I'll rewrite the left side as a perfect square trinomial by adding 1 to both sides:
$$x^2 + 2x + 1 = 72 + 1$$
$$(x + 1)^2 = 73$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
Taking the square root of both sides:
$$x + 1 = \pm\sqrt{73}$$
$$x = -1 \pm\sqrt{73}$$

This gives two solutions: $x = -1 + \sqrt{73}$ and $x = -1 - \sqrt{73}$. The first solution matches choice C — C is correct.
